parameters.text:
=Generate the HTML email for this data:
{{ JSON.stringify($json) }}

messageValues.message:
You are an HTML email generator for a weekly data quality audit report on Shopify order data.

OUTPUT: Raw HTML only. No markdown, no backticks, no explanation.

RULES:
- All CSS must be inline. No <style> blocks.
- Single column, max-width 600px, centered, font-family: Arial, sans-serif.
- Background: #f4f4f4 outer, #ffffff inner card.

STRUCTURE — follow this order exactly:

[HEADER]
- If status is "✅ ALL CLEAR": dark green background (#1E3A2F), white text.
- If status is "⚠ ANOMALIES DETECTED": dark red background (#7A1F1F), white text.
- Title: "Weekly Audit Report"
- Subtitle: "Sheet: {sheet_name} · {audit_timestamp}"

[SUMMARY GRID]
2 rows × 3 columns, thin gray borders (#e5e5e5) between cells.
Row 1: Total rows | Total orders | Duplicate line items (red if > 0)
Row 2: Financial anomalies (red if > 0) | Data issues (red if > 0) | Order inconsistencies (red if > 0)
Each cell: label uppercase 11px gray, value 22px dark.

[ANOMALY SECTIONS]
Only render a section if its array is not empty.

Section "Duplicate Line Items" — table: line_item_id | order_number | product_name | occurrences

Section "Financial Anomalies" — table: row | order_number | product_name | issue

Section "Data Issues" — table: row | order_number | customer_name | product_name | issues (join with comma)

Section "Order Inconsistencies" — table: row | order_number | issue | expected | found

Section "Wrong Week Rows" — table: row | order_number | inserted_at | issue

Section "Custom Item Inconsistencies" — table: row | order_number | product_name | issue

Each section title: bold, 13px, dark, uppercase, with a colored left border (#E24B4A), padding 8px 12px, background #fff5f5.
Each table: full width, font-size 12px, alternating row background (#ffffff / #f9f9f9), header row background #f0f0f0.

[ALL CLEAR BLOCK]
Only render if all anomaly arrays are empty.
Centered div, green text (#1D9E75), font-size 14px: "✅ No anomalies detected this week. Data integrity confirmed."

[FOOTER]
Centered, muted gray (#888780), 11px.
"Generated automatically by ali-n8n.com"

COLOR CONSTANTS:
Header OK: #1E3A2F | Header alert: #7A1F1F | Anomaly accent: #E24B4A
All clear: #1D9E75 | Border: #e5e5e5 | Muted: #888780

parameters.subject: ={{ $('Weekly Sheet Quality Audit').first().json.status === '✅ ALL CLEAR' ? '✅' : '⚠️' }} Audit {{ $('Weekly Sheet Quality Audit').first().json.sheet_name }} — {{ $('Weekly Sheet Quality Audit').first().json.summary.financial_anomalies + $('Weekly Sheet Quality Audit').first().json.summary.data_issues + $('Weekly Sheet Quality Audit').first().json.summary.duplicate_line_items }} anomalie(s) sur {{ $('Weekly Sheet Quality Audit').first().json.summary.total_rows }} lignes · {{ $('Weekly Sheet Quality Audit').first().json.summary.total_orders }} commandes

parameters.content:
# WF-02 — Weekly Sheet Quality Controller

## Purpose
Weekly automated audit of Shopify order data stored in Google Sheets.
Every Monday at 06:00 UTC, analyzes the previous ISO week tab and delivers
a structured HTML report via email.

## Trigger
Schedule : every Monday at 06:00 UTC
Manual   : available for on-demand testing

## Execution Flow

1. Compute Previous Week Sheet Name (Code)
   Computes target tab name: WEEK{n-1}_{year} (now − 7 days, UTC).
   ⚠️ Uses ISO week year, not calendar year.
      Week 1 of 2027 can start in late December 2026 — this is correct.

2. Get line items from Sheet (Google Sheets)
   Reads all rows from the target tab in ShopifyPaidOrders_Spreadsheet.
   Retry: 2 attempts, 5s apart.
   ⚠️ If the tab does not exist, this node errors — it does not create it.
      Tab creation is handled exclusively by WF-01 Track A.

3. Weekly Sheet Quality Audit (Code)
   → See dedicated sticky "Audit Engine — Code Node"

4. Audit Report Email Generator (LLM Chain)
   Sends audit JSON to LLM → returns raw inline-CSS HTML.
   Primary  : Grok 4 Fast (OpenRouter)
   Fallback : Claude Haiku 4.5 (needsFallback: true, auto-activates on failure)
   Max tokens : 6000. System prompt enforces raw HTML only — no markdown, no backticks.
   ⚠️ If anomaly arrays exceed ~100 rows, truncate before this node
      to avoid token overflow.

5. Send audit report (Gmail)
   Sends HTML report to aliboussechill@gmail.com.
   Subject: sheet name / total anomalies / row count / order count.
   Retry: 2 attempts, 5s apart.
   onError: continueErrorOutput → Telegram fallback → StopAndError

## Telegram Fallback (disabled by default)
Reached only if Gmail fails.
To enable:
  1. Set chatId to your real Telegram chat or group ID
  2. Adjust message template if needed
  3. Toggle the node ON
⚠️ StopAndError fires regardless to surface Gmail failures in execution logs.

## Error Handling
Global error workflow : gvhDaCea1oROHUrb
Gmail node           : onError continueErrorOutput → Telegram → StopAndError

## Dependencies
  Redis         → shopify:sheet:name (previous week tab resolution)
  Google Sheets → ShopifyPaidOrders_Spreadsheet
  Gmail OAuth2  → aliboussechill@gmail.com
  OpenRouter    → Grok 4 Fast / Claude Haiku 4.5

Author: Ali Boussecsou — ali-n8n.com

## Meta
Version     : 1.0.0
Status      : stable — production
Last update : 2026-05
n8n version : 2.15.1
Depends on  : WF-01 (tab creation), Redis (sheet name state)

Changelog:
  1.0.0 — Initial production release
    • 7-check pure JS audit engine
    • LLM report generation with primary/fallback chain
    • Gmail delivery + Telegram fallback (disabled by default)
    • ISO 8601 previous-week targeting (UTC)

## Audit Engine — Weekly Sheet Quality Audit

Pure JS code node. Zero external dependencies.
Input  : all rows from the target weekly tab
Output : single JSON object

Output schema:
  status          → "ALL CLEAR" | "ANOMALIES DETECTED"
  summary         → { check_name: flagged_count, ... } for all 7 checks
  [per-category]  → array of flagged rows with context fields

## 7 Checks

┌───┬──────────────────────────────┬──────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ # │ Check                        │ Flag condition                           │
├───┼──────────────────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ 1 │ Duplicate line_item_id       │ Same ID appears more than once           │
│ 2 │ Duplicate order_id           │ Full order written more than once        │
│ 3 │ Financial consistency        │ unit_price × qty ≠ total_line_price      │
│   │                              │ tolerance ±0.02 (Shopify float rounding) │
│ 4 │ Missing critical fields      │ Empty values in required columns         │
│ 5 │ Customer consistency         │ Conflicting metadata within same order   │
│ 6 │ Date out of window           │ inserted_at outside expected ISO week    │
│ 7 │ is_custom_item vs SKU        │ Custom flag inconsistent with SKU value  │
└───┴──────────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────────────────┘

## Tuneable Constants
  PRICE_THRESHOLD = 50000
    Flags unit_price above this value as a financial anomaly.
    Adjust if your catalogue includes legitimate high-ticket items.

  Price math tolerance = ±0.02
    Covers Shopify float rounding on line totals.
    Do not reduce — will produce false positives.
